why: A non-`default` stack MUST round-trip into the 6-segment stack-aware form. Guards the sibling of cortex#1812 (a real stack must not be lost).
public. subjects carry NEITHER principal NOR stack; the identity fields MUST be ignored (D10; specs/namespace.md:57-59; deriveSubject :618-620).

With stack omitted under an EXPLICIT legacy:true opt-in, the 5-segment migration form is emitted (transitional, D17). Contrast legacy/reject-silent-stackless-emit: absent-stack with NO opt-in is a reject (D18). This form is subject-matching compat only; retirement is BCP-0001's.

MASKING CASE. With no hint the wire form is undecidable (stack=default,type=tasks.chat) vs (type=default.tasks.chat); detectSubjectForm defaults to `legacy` and the fabricated `default` coincidentally matches — the exact silent failure that produced cortex#1812's `default`-derivation defect.
The out-of-band stack hint resolves the §3.3 ambiguity to stack-aware. Precision on the stack-aware path REQUIRES a hint (src/subjects.ts:799-801).

ROOT CAUSE cortex#1812. The unsigned subject stack is NEVER authoritative (D6/D7 signed-wins); an absent stack MUST NOT be fabricated into `default` for identity resolution / roster / stack-id (§8). Fault, never a default.

PRE-CUT flat-DID encode (byte-mapping ':' → '-'). `did:mf:forge` is CLASSLESS; under RFC-0001's class-explicit dot-form it is REJECTED at parse from flag-day R (RFC-0001 inv/legacy-classless). This vector pins the pre-cut encoder byte-behaviour; the class-explicit successor is encode/agent-dotform-subject (D25).

'.' → '--'. Under RFC-0001's class-explicit dot-form this is a VALID hub-class DID (tag=hub), so the encode SURVIVES the cut; it stays DISTINCT from the now-pre-cut did:mf:hub-metafactory below — the '.'/'-' distinction was the injectivity boundary (a real security-boundary violation, specs/namespace.md:236).

PRE-CUT. '-' preserved. `did:mf:hub-metafactory` is the CLASSLESS flat form — REJECTED at parse from flag-day R (RFC-0001 inv/legacy-classless). Pins the pre-cut encoder byte-behaviour; distinct from the valid hub-class did:mf:hub.metafactory above (D25).

PRE-CUT NON-INJECTIVITY COLLISION (half 1). did:mf:a-.b and did:mf:a.-b BOTH encoded to @did-mf-a---b under the flat grammar — the bare injectivity claim was FALSE for '-.'/'.-' adjacency. OD-1 RESOLVED by RFC-0001 (class-explicit dot-form + kebab-strict, cortex#1880): from flag-day R both inputs are UNMINTABLE (segment-edge hyphens rejected); the vector pins the pre-cut defect (D25).

PRE-CUT NON-INJECTIVITY COLLISION (half 2). Encoded byte-identically to did:mf:a-.b — two distinct assistants became one wire address; the '---' run is ambiguous ('-'+'--' vs '--'+'-'). OD-1 RESOLVED by RFC-0001: post-R both inputs unmintable under kebab-strict; the vector pins the pre-cut defect (D25).

The @-segment charset excludes '_'. Pins the PRE-CUT leak: encodeDidSegment('did:mf:a_b') PRODUCED this invalid segment because the flat DID_RE admitted '_' and the encoder passed it through. Co-owned with RFC-0001; RESOLVED — kebab-strict forbids '_' entirely, so post-R no encoder can emit it (RFC-0001 inv/underscore).
Seed-taxonomy tag: single interior hyphen, ends alnum, 2-64 chars — valid under CAPABILITY_TAG_RE (src/patterns.ts:21).

CAPABILITY_TAG_RE requires >=2 chars; single-char tags collide with 1-char structured identifiers. But taskSubject/offerTaskSubject accept 'x' via STACK_SEGMENT_REGEX (1-char legal) — emitter-dependent validity (FINDING §7.1). The strict grammar (canonical Offer position-4 tag, D16) governs.
CAPABILITY_TAG_RE bans trailing '-'; STACK_SEGMENT_REGEX permits it. 'scan-' is valid to taskSubject but invalid to bidRequestSubject — the three-way capability drift (FINDING §7.1).

`dead-letter` is a RESERVED tasks position-4 tag (D16); a capability tag MUST NOT equal it (specs/namespace.md:172-174). DEFECT: no code rejects it — 'dead-letter' matches CAPABILITY_TAG_RE and taskDeadLetterSubject merely no-ops (src/subjects.ts:529-531), so escalation subjects are spoofable (FINDING §7.3). Pairs with tasks/reject-capability-equals-bid-request.

Published subjects MUST be fully qualified (specs/namespace.md:130). DEFECT: subjectFor/deriveSubject skip segment validation on principal/type and return 'local.*.x.y' (audit subjects/derive-helpers-skip-grammar-validation, FINDING §7.3).
Stack-aware dead-letter derivation MUST preserve the stack and the first capability segment (src/subjects.ts:508-535).

COLLISION: a stack literally named 'tasks'. DEFECT: taskDeadLetterSubject checks parts[2]==='tasks' with LEGACY priority, misparsing the stack-aware subject as legacy and dropping the stack (audit subjects/stack-named-tasks-misparse, FINDING §7.8). Vector states the correct expectation.

The metrics {token} tail is LOWERCASE-ONLY (D26; specs/namespace.md:107). DEFECT: sanitizeSubjectToken preserves A-Z, emitting 'local.acme._metrics.transport.MyApp' (audit subjects/metrics-subject-uppercase-leak, FINDING §7.7). Accept-half: metrics/accept-lowercase-token.
Prefix<->classification alignment is the ONLY subject<->envelope check implemented (src/subjects.ts:703-722); a runtime guard, not a grammar property (FINDING §7.9 / SEC).
A prefix/classification mismatch is a protocol violation (specs/namespace.md:341). Enforced by a runtime transport guard, not by the subject format.

Total subject MUST be <=255 chars (specs/namespace.md:111). DEFECT: no code path measures composed length anywhere in src/ (audit subjects/total-255-cap-unenforced, FINDING §7.6); over-long subjects surface as opaque NATS errors.
D10: `local` is one of the CLOSED 3-prefix set; a stack-aware local subject is accepted. The TERMINAL grammar mandates the stack segment (D17) — this is the terminal form.
D10: `federated` is a closed-set prefix; the subject MAY cross the principal boundary and is subject to the envelope sovereignty block (RFC-0003).
D10: public. carries NO principal and NO stack; on-wire it is UNATTRIBUTED — origin is established ONLY by the signed_by chain (RFC-0003), NEVER by the subject (§8.3 security note).

D10/§2: the classification set { local, federated, public } is CLOSED; a first segment that is none of the three (nor a reserved '_'-prefix) is malformed and MUST be rejected (D27). A 4th prefix requires a new RFC.
D27/§2: a local./federated. subject MUST carry a principal-body (principal + type); a bare classification token with no body is malformed.
D11 OPEN-WITH-RESERVED-ROOTS: `telemetry` is a NON-reserved domain root and is freely usable; the domain slot is open and not closeable across M7.
D11/D16: `tasks` is a reserved root; the Offer shape is tasks.{capability}.{subcapability}; position-4 capability `code-review` is the canonical tagged-union member.
D12: review.verdict.{kind}.{status} is the CANONICAL verdict shape; reserves the `review` root; retargets the pre-cut code.pr.{kind}.{status} (retired at flag-day R).
D13: `brain` is a reserved domain root; NORMATIVE wire invariant — brain.> and tasks.> are STREAM-DISJOINT (every dual-stream peer respects it); stream provisioning stays M7-owned.

D11: `dispatch` is a reserved root that FAILS CLOSED against application misuse; `dispatch.custom.thing` is not a valid dispatch shape (dispatch.task.{state} / dispatch.bid.{event} / dispatch.{workflow-event}), so an application MUST NOT emit it.

D25 class-explicit dot-form: the WHOLE agent DID rides into the subject (':' → '-', '.' → '--'); the interior '-' of meta-factory passes through verbatim. Injective under kebab-strict (no segment-edge '-', so '--' can only come from '.'). Subject-plane successor to the pre-cut encode/did-forge.
D25 round-trip back: split the encoded msi on '--', rejoin with '.'; the single '-' inside meta-factory is NOT a separator. decodeDidSegment is total and injective on this language (RFC-0001).
D1: the Direct/Delegate @-address encodes the WHOLE class-explicit agent DID, REPEATING the subject's own {principal}.{stack} (andreas/meta-factory) — a full DID, NOT a prefix-relative projection.

D1 BLOCKING recipient-security gate: the @-segment byte-EQUALS encodeDidSegment(target_assistant). Full-DID match is zero-code-delta — the deployed publisher already builds the whole DID (validateCanonicalTaskRecipient, dispatch-listener.ts:2399-2439).

D1: the gate byte-compares the @-segment against encodeDidSegment(target_assistant) and HARD-DROPS on mismatch (@did-mf-...luna != @did-mf-...echo). This is the security property the full-DID @-segment exists to enforce — a projection could not serve it.
D2: the @-address is EXEMPT from the 63-octet per-segment cap; this 88-octet @-segment is LEGAL because each INNER msi segment is <=63 and only the 255-octet TOTAL-subject budget binds.
D2: the 63-cap STILL binds PER INNER msi segment (RFC-0001) — the 64-char principal segment exceeds it. Only the WHOLE @-segment is exempt, never its inner segments.
D20: a leading '_' is the UNIVERSAL structural reservation marker; an APPLICATION publisher MUST NOT emit a reserved-prefix subject. The '_'-space is infrastructure-only.

D22: `_INBOX.` is admitted BY REFERENCE to RFC-0007 (the NATS request-reply inbox); uppercase-EXEMPT (NATS's own byte-for-byte string); RFC-0007 owns the tail grammar (inbox-id), referenced not redefined.
D21: sovereignty enforcement-NAKs FOLD under `_audit.sovereignty.*` (RFC-0005) — there is NO separate top-level `_nak.` prefix. Minimizes the reserved-infra wall.

D26: the metrics {token} tail is LOWERCASE-ONLY and a lowercase token is accepted (accept-half of metrics/reject-uppercase). `_metrics` reaches its reserved DOMAIN slot via the D20 '_'-space carve-out (supersedes the former 'no carve-out' FINDING §7.7).

D27/§3.1: an ordinary segment MUST be <=63 octets; a 64-char principal is rejected. Contrast the @-address exemption (atsegment/exempt-from-63-cap) — only the @-segment is exempt from the per-segment cap.

D28 positive half: the TASKS stream filter binds the stack-aware shape. The pattern is ANCHORED under the literal `local`; '*' fills the principal + stack positions; the '>' tail stays WITHIN the local scope.
D28/§4: an offer subscription tasks.{capability}.> reaches a sub-capability subject because '>' matches the trailing `typescript`.

D28/§4: '>' matches ONE-OR-MORE trailing segments, so a TERMINAL 4-segment tasks capability subject (no sub-capability) is UNREACHABLE from the offer subscription — offer-reachability is encoded purely in segment count.

D24: a bare '>' at the classification position crosses the scope boundary (local+federated+public) AND into the '_'-space; the grammar MUST NOT express it (security-first can't-express). Every sub-pattern is anchored under a literal classification.
D24: the classification position can be NEITHER '*' NOR '>'; it MUST be a literal prefix (local / federated / public).

D24: a '>' MUST NOT descend into the top-level '_'-space; reserved-space subscriptions are infra-only and are NOT expressible in the application sub-pattern grammar (the '_'-space is a SIBLING of the classifications, unreachable from an anchored sub-pattern).

D18: reject = MUST-not-emit at EVERY derivation entry point. An absent stack with NO explicit legacy:true opt-in MUST be rejected (closes the deriveSubject silent-emit primitive gap, subjects.ts:622; subjectFor already throws, :673). The legacy/stack-aware choice is never made silently. Contrast subject/derive-legacy-stackless (explicit opt-in accepted).
D16: `bid-request` is now a REGISTERED reserved tasks position-4 tag (closes OD-5); a capability MUST NOT equal it (fail-closed), just as capability/reject-dead-letter.
D16: bid-request is REGISTERED as a tasks position-4 tag; its shape is tasks.bid-request.{capability}.

D14: `dispatched` is the CANONICAL lifecycle token (received -> dispatched -> started -> completed -> aborted/failed); it RETARGETS the pre-cut `assigned`. RFC-0002 owns the shape; the closed enum is homed in a dispatch-observability RFC.

D15/D29 seam hygiene: the RFC-0008 capability-id `federated.subject_dispatch` (dotted, underscore-bearing) is NOT expressible as a subject-position capability tag — both '.' and '_' are rejected by CAPABILITY_TAG_RE. One owner per wire rule: RFC-0008 owns the capability-id, RFC-0002 owns the subject-position tag; RFC-0008 MUST constrain its id charset to be subject-safe (charset intersect segment).

D15: a subject-safe capability-id projects 1:1 into the tasks slot (RFC-0002 owns the projection). The seam holds when RFC-0008 constrains its id charset to the subject-safe set — the deliberate resolution of the '_' incompatibility.

D29/§7.2 drift pair: the subject principal and the identity-plane principal-id DRIFT on the trailing hyphen (the deployed subject regex permits `acme-`; RFC-0001 principal-id forbids it). BOTH tighten to kebab-strict at flag-day R; the vector states the tightened (R) rejection — single kebab-strict owner (RFC-0001).
